apply.title: Apply for B40 Education Assistance
apply.intro: Financial assistance for B40 students continuing to IPTA/ILKA. Read the criteria, then complete the short form below.
apply.criteria1: Household monthly income within the B40 band (STR / supporting documents).
apply.criteria2: At least 5 A's in SPM, or a PNGK of 3.0 or above in STPM.
apply.criteria3: Intending to continue to tertiary education this year.
apply.criteria4: Willing to be contacted about your application.

tip.name: Your full name as in your IC.
tip.school: The school where you sat for SPM or STPM.
tip.ic: Used to confirm your identity. You can edit it until it's verified.
tip.org: The organisation or coordinator who told you about this programme.
tip.state: The state where you normally live. We save this to your profile when you submit, so you only need to update it in one place.
tip.phone: A number we can reach you on. Format: 01X-XXX XXXX.
tip.income: Add up the monthly income of everyone you counted above — salaries, business, pensions, and government aid (not just your parents'). An estimate is fine.
tip.household: Count everyone who normally lives in your home and shares income or expenses — your parents/guardians, you, your brothers and sisters, and grandparents or relatives living with you. Include a working sibling if they live at home and contribute; don't count family who live elsewhere. Next, you'll total this group's monthly income.
tip.str: Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah — government cash aid for lower-income households.
tip.jkm: Help from Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat (the welfare department).
tip.parentPhone: A number for your parent or guardian.

tip.otherScholarships: Tell us if you already hold or applied for other funding — it helps us prioritise.
tip.helpUniversity: Choosing courses, filling forms, personal statements.

help.ic: A photo of the front of your MyKad. We'll check it automatically to help you spot typos — your photo isn't kept at Google.

consent.draftNotice: DRAFT — final wording pending legal review.
consent.text: I consent to the B40 Assistance Programme sharing my profile and supporting information with potential sponsors for the purpose of considering me for financial assistance. I understand I can withdraw this consent at any time. We may also run automated OCR (text recognition) on the documents you upload to help spot typos; the results are a soft signal only and never replace human review.
consent.guardianNotice: As you are under 18, a parent or guardian must give this consent.
consent.agree: I have read and agree to the above.
consent.agreeGuardian: As guardian, I have read and agree to the above on the applicant's behalf.

hero.heading: A community behind every student.
hero.sub: A community-supported pilot helping students from low-income households continue their studies at Malaysia's public colleges and universities — from pre-university through to degree — backed by fellow Malaysians who want to see them succeed.

overview.p1: Every year, promising students from low-income households earn a place at Malaysian public institutions — to pursue Pre-university studies, Certificate, Diploma and Degree — only to find they lack the means to make the most of it. This programme exists to close that gap.
overview.p2: The model is straightforward. Members of the community choose to support a student through their studies. It is assistance, not a loan — there is nothing to repay. Our hope is that those who are supported today will, in time, support others tomorrow.

card1.title: Realise your potential
card1.desc: Practical support to help you make the most of your place at a public institution.

card2.title: A community behind you
card2.desc: Funded by individuals, not a faceless institution, who want you to succeed.

card3.title: Your privacy, your control
card3.desc: Sponsors only see what you choose to share.

note.title: Please note
note.pilotText: Expect some uncertainty as we get going. We'll be transparent about what we don't know yet.
note.noGuaranteeText: Applying lets us assess your eligibility. It does not guarantee assistance.
note.limitedText: We try to support every eligible student, but places in this pilot are limited.
note.accurateText: Honest answers help us match you to opportunities you're genuinely eligible for, and protect places for those who need them.
note.verifyText: We take your application in good faith. We may ask for supporting documents to confirm key details.
note.under18Text: A parent or guardian must give consent on your behalf before your profile is shared with any sponsor.
note.confidentialText: Your answers are shared only with the programme coordinators, the administering NGO, and any sponsor you specifically consent to.

req.title: Can I apply?
req.item1: Malaysian citizen who has just completed, or is completing, SPM or STPM.
req.item2: From a low-income household — combined monthly income at or below RM5,860, the national B40 threshold (DOSM, 2024).
req.item3: Solid academic record — at least 5 A's in SPM, or a PNGK of 3.0 or above in STPM.
req.item4: Continuing at a Malaysian public institution — Asasi, Matrikulasi, STPM, Certificate, Diploma or Degree.
req.item5: Reachable for a short interview — usually a 20-minute phone call in English, Bahasa Malaysia, or Tamil.
req.item6: Willing to share progress — semester results and a brief update, so that support can continue through your studies.
req.closeNote: Close, but not exactly? Apply anyway. Our bar is a little more generous than the headline above — especially on grades — so it's worth applying even if you don't match every line exactly. Applying lets us assess your eligibility; it doesn't guarantee assistance.

how.title: How it works
how.rolling: No fixed deadline — reviewed on a rolling basis
how.step1Desc: Sign in with Google, confirm your HalaTuju profile, and submit. About 5–10 minutes.
how.step2Desc: You'll receive an email acknowledging your application on the day you submit it.
how.step3Desc: We check your results and household income against the criteria and let you know whether you've been shortlisted.
how.step4Desc: If shortlisted, we'll ask you to share a few more details and upload supporting documents (IC, results slip, proof of household income, etc.). We'll tell you exactly what's needed.
how.step5Desc: A brief phone call, usually 20 minutes, in your preferred language. This is to get to know you, not to test you.
how.step6Desc: With your written consent, we prepare a confidential profile for sponsors to consider.
how.step7Desc: Once a sponsor chooses to support you, Yayasan myNADI arranges the assistance and administers the funds. From application to award typically takes up to two months. (Partnership being finalised.)
how.step8Desc: Each semester, you upload your results and a short progress note. As long as you're progressing, the support continues.

faq.title: Questions
faq.q1: Do I need to have used HalaTuju before?
faq.a1: No. If you're new, the same Google sign-in creates your account. If you've already done the HalaTuju course-matching quiz, we'll use what's in your profile so you don't have to retype anything.
faq.q2: Is this a loan? Do I have to pay it back?
faq.a2: No. It is education assistance from a sponsor — nothing to repay, no interest. Our hope is that those who are supported today will, in time, support others tomorrow.
faq.q3: Is the assistance guaranteed if I qualify?
faq.a3: No. This is a pilot with limited places and strict criteria. Applying lets us assess you; it does not guarantee assistance.
faq.q4: Who can see my personal details?
faq.a4: Your private details — your IC, contact details, and home address — are never shown to sponsors. Sponsors only see a profile, and only after you have given your consent. If you are under 18, a parent or guardian must give that consent on your behalf.
faq.a5: Only after you've been shortlisted: your IC, your SPM or STPM results slip, and proof of household income or aid (such as your STR letter, an EPF statement, or a payslip). We'll tell you exactly what's needed at that stage — there is nothing to upload when you first apply.
faq.a6: Public post-secondary study only — both pre-university (Asasi at a university or Polytechnic, Matrikulasi, or STPM) and tertiary (at a University, Polytechnic, Community College, IPGM, ILJTM or ILKBS).
faq.a7: No. The programme supports study at public institutions only. Foundation or Asasi programmes must also be at a public institution (IPTA) to qualify.
faq.a8: Not at present — applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. You'll receive an acknowledgement on the same day you apply, and we aim to confirm shortlisting within 48 hours. From application to being matched with a sponsor typically takes up to two months.
faq.q9: Is there a cost to apply?
faq.a9: No. Applying is free and takes about 5–10 minutes.

donor.title: Want to support a student?
donor.body: This programme exists because individuals in the community choose to fund the studies of specific students. If you'd like to know more about supporting a student — whether for a semester, a year, or the full course of their studies — we'd be glad to hear from you.
donor.fundsPost: , a Malaysian non-profit organisation with Tax Exemption under Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967. (Partnership being finalised.)

about.title: About this programme
about.body: The B40 Assistance Programme is coordinated by HalaTuju, with Yayasan myNADI expected to administer the funds (partnership being finalised), and a network of community organisations — including Sri Murugan Centre (SMC), Concerned Universiti Malaya Indian Graduates (CUMIG) and others — that refer and support students.

cta.heading: Ready to apply?
cta.body: It takes about 5–10 minutes, and we'll use what's already in your HalaTuju profile.

pathwayDetail.stpmCaveat: School data is from MOE SST6 portal. Offerings may change each intake.

about.problemP1: Every year, thousands of Malaysian students leave school and enrol in expensive private colleges — often taking on heavy student loans for qualifications that don't always match what's available for free or at heavily subsidised rates in the public system.
about.problemP2: In 2024, over 137,000 places were available across polytechnics, community colleges, TVET institutions, and public universities — not including UiTM. These are government-funded, industry-recognised programmes. But too many students and families never find them, because the information is scattered and hard to navigate.
about.whatDesc: Enter your SPM or STPM results, and our eligibility engine checks them against the entry requirements of 1,300+ courses across 800+ public institutions in Malaysia. We help you find the programmes you're actually eligible for — matched to your grades, your interests, and your goals.
about.whoP1: HalaTuju is built under Project Lentera, a community-driven initiative focused on improving access to public tertiary education for Malaysian students.
about.whoP2: This is a volunteer effort — no single person owns it. Teachers, counsellors, parents, alumni, and community organisations like Yayasan myNADI and Concerned UM Indian Graduates (CUMIG) all play a part. We believe every student deserves to make an informed choice, not a default one driven by lack of information.
about.helpParentsDesc: Use HalaTuju with your child after SPM or STPM results. The options may surprise you.
about.helpTeachersDesc: Share it with your students. Five minutes could save a family years of debt.
about.helpEveryoneDesc: Spread the word. The more families who know about public options, the fewer who choose private ones simply because they didn't know better.
about.helpClosing: HalaTuju is a growing platform — we're continuously adding courses and improving recommendations. If you have feedback or want to contribute, we want to hear from you.

stpm.noResultsDesc: Try adjusting your search or filters

matric-sains.headline: Fast Track to University for Science Students
matric-sains.description: A 1–2 year matriculation programme under KPM that prepares you for a bachelor's degree in science, medicine, pharmacy, and related fields. You study Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology at pre-university level — more rigorous than SPM but with lecturer support and tutorials. Admission is based on SPM merit and quota allocation.

matric-kejuruteraan.headline: A Strong Foundation for an Engineering Career
matric-kejuruteraan.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme focused on Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry — the three pillars of engineering. You get early exposure to engineering concepts before progressing to a bachelor's degree at a public university. Ideal for students who enjoy solving technical problems and building things.

matric-sains-komputer.headline: Start Your Career in Information Technology
matric-sains-komputer.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme covering programming fundamentals, discrete mathematics, and introduction to computer systems. Designed for students who want to pursue a degree in computer science, software engineering, or information technology at a public university.

matric-perakaunan.headline: Your First Step into Finance and Accounting
matric-perakaunan.description: A 1–2 year KPM matriculation programme providing foundations in accounting principles, economics, and business mathematics. Prepares you for a bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or business management at a public university. Suited for students who are detail-oriented with numbers and interested in the business world.

stpm-sains.headline: An Internationally Recognised Pre-U Qualification
stpm-sains.description: STPM Science is an 18-month programme (3 terms) at Form 6, equivalent to A-Levels. You choose 3–4 science subjects (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology) and sit examinations each term. STPM results are accepted by public and private universities locally and internationally. The best option if you want an open pathway to a wide range of universities.

stpm-sains-sosial.headline: A Flexible Pathway to Arts, Law, and Business
stpm-sains-sosial.description: STPM Social Science is an 18-month programme (3 terms) at Form 6 covering subjects like Economics, Business Studies, History, Geography, and Literature. An A-Level equivalent qualification that opens doors to degrees in law, public administration, communications, and more. Suited for students who enjoy analysing social issues and thinking critically.

courses.descriptionFallback: This {level} programme in {field} prepares students for careers in {department}. The course combines theoretical knowledge with practical skills to ensure graduates are industry-ready.
